message:
Last winter, my kid asked why we had those flashlights in the hall closet. I almost said "just in case," but instead we actually *tested* them together. Turns out one was dead, one was dim, and one worked great.

We swapped in fresh batteries that afternoon—no big deal, five minutes tops.

Then a ice storm knocked out power for 18 hours. And you know what? We had *light* when we needed it. Not panic, not scrambling in the dark, just... light. My kid felt safer. I felt calmer. A small thing, but it changed the whole vibe of the outage.

I've learned that the best "prep" isn't about stockpiling or stressing—it's about little checkups that actually work. Testing what you have, refreshing what's old, knowing your stuff *works* before the weather gets weird.

Do you have a small-win prep moment that surprised you?
